era: 1990-1995. Grunge/Metal. E=8. Seattle. Roadrunner Records. Ben McMillan (vocals) + Tom Niemeyer (guitar): Skin Yard alumni. Post-Skin Yard formation. The heaviest of the second-tier grunge acts. Debut Enter Sandman competition context. Short career, high energy floor.
members: Ben McMillan (vocals, born May 8 1966 Seattle WA, died January 14 2008 Seattle WA, age 41)
key_albums: Enter Sandman EP (1992, Roadrunner) -- debut; recorded during Metallica's Black Album campaign; early Seattle metal-grunge bridge
Gruntruck (1992, Roadrunner) -- self-titled full length; In My Hands, Tribe, Crucifunken
Pushed Again (1993, Roadrunner) -- final record; expanded sonic range

theory_nodes.concept: SKIN YARD LINEAGE. Skin Yard (1985-1992, Seattle) was one of the scene's earliest metal-adjacent acts, predating the major grunge commercial breakthrough. Ben McMillan and Tom Niemeyer from Skin Yard: Gruntruck carries the heavy metal foundation that Skin Yard developed in the pre-commercial-grunge era. The Skin Yard -> Gruntruck line is the engine's heaviest pre-major-label-era lineage.
theory_nodes.description: Gruntruck as the Skin Yard continuity thread: the metal foundation beneath early Seattle grunge.

theory_nodes.concept: E=8 CALIBRATION. Gruntruck is the engine's second entry (after Melvins, degree 308) to reach E=8 from the metal side of grunge rather than the melodic side. The Roadrunner Records signing is the calibration signal: Roadrunner in 1992 was the primary metal label (Sepultura, Obituary, Type O Negative). Gruntruck's signing places them in metal company, not alternative-rock company. The metal floor sustains E=8 through the full catalog.
theory_nodes.description: E=8 from metal genealogy (Roadrunner, Skin Yard) rather than grunge cultural adjacency.

theory_nodes.concept: BEN McMILLAN (May 8 1966 - January 14 2008). Born and died Seattle. Age 41. McMillan's post-Gruntruck project: Skin Yard reunion (brief) and Deathride (unreleased material). Died January 14 2008 of insulin-related complications. The engine documents his death factually, parallel to the documentation of Staley (313) and Hawkins (315). The pattern of loss in the Seattle scene continues through the second tier.
theory_nodes.description: Ben McMillan: biography and death documented with factual respect.

theory_nodes.concept: RETURN CONTEXT: 'return' in the degree slug references the engine's prior Gruntruck documentation (an earlier degree established them). This degree deepens the analysis, completing the Gruntruck arc with the full discography, McMillan biography, and the Skin Yard lineage in detail. The E=8 calibration is confirmed from the additional discographic evidence of Gruntruck (1992) and Pushed Again (1993).
theory_nodes.description: Degree 316 as a return entry that completes the Gruntruck analysis from an earlier engine appearance.

theory_nodes.concept: ROADRUNNER RECORDS CONTEXT (1992): Roadrunner in 1992 was completing Sepultura's Chaos A.D. (1993), had signed Type O Negative (Bloody Kisses, 1993), distributed Obituary, and was the catalog-building phase before the nu-metal surge. Gruntruck's 1992 Roadrunner position places them precisely at the intersection of Seattle grunge and East Coast/international metal. The label context is a calibration anchor -- Roadrunner did not sign melodic alternative acts.
theory_nodes.description: Roadrunner 1992 roster as calibration anchor for Gruntruck's E=8 metal positioning.

theory_nodes.concept: CRUCIFUNKEN (Gruntruck, 1992): the most tonally heavy track in the self-titled LP. The riff architecture draws from Skin Yard's earlier dense-chord approach and from the general metal vocabulary of the early 1990s. The track title -- a portmanteau of 'crucify' and 'funken' (a James Brown-adjacent suffix) -- signals the band's deliberate genre hybridization: metal weight, groove influence, Seattle production. The hybridization is the engine's documentation target.
theory_nodes.description: Crucifunken and In My Hands as the Gruntruck hybrid-genre signature tracks.

cross_references.relationship: Consecutive post-Summit entries at E=8 and E=7 respectively. Gruntruck (316, E=8) recalibrates upward from Foo Fighters (315, E=7): the metal floor of the second-tier grunge acts is higher than the post-grunge melodic register.

Both E=8 from metal genealogy. Melvins: Aberdeen sludge metal. Gruntruck: Seattle metal-grunge via Roadrunner. Both entries at E=8 through metal, not through melodic or cultural grunge factors. The metal E=8 pair in the engine.
This is a return entry. Earlier degree established Gruntruck's place in the engine; degree 316 completes the analysis with full discography and McMillan biography.

Skin Yard (1985-1992) predates Gruntruck and will enter the engine in a future degree. The Skin Yard -> Gruntruck lineage is documented here; the Skin Yard entry will precede it chronologically in the engine's historical arc.

sps_pair.species: Steller Sea Lion bull apex roar
sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Gruntruck E=8 (metal floor, Roadrunner genealogy, the heaviest second-tier grunge act) paired with Steller Sea Lion bull apex roar E=8 (the largest otariid, maximum body mass vocalization at haul-out). Both E=8 through body/sound mass at maximum -- McMillan's voice at full metal weight, the bull sea lion's roar from maximum body mass. Mass and weight as E=8 generators.
